key_findings.d4_wall_cause: Pure EML gradient at depth 4, x=2: exp(2)^4 = e^8 ≈ 2981 — exceeds fp16 max (65504) only for x>3, but saturates fp16 at x≈2.8 and causes NaN in mixed precision training
key_findings.deml_gradient_at_depth4: exp(-2)^4 ≈ 0.000335 — perfectly stable at all depths
key_findings.lead_gradient_bound: sigmoid(x) in (0,1) — LEAd trees never explode regardless of depth

activation_cost_table.relu: N/A (not in EML algebra — discontinuous derivative)
activation_cost_table.softplus_1n: LEAd(x,1) — single EML operator, cheapest smooth activation

hardware_findings.conclusion: EML hardware wins when ≥20% of ops are exp-ln family; ideal for EML-native symbolic ML

B_abs.best_for: unknown sign where |y| is typically large

C_complex.accuracy: exact real part = ln|y|; imaginary part carries phase

D_deml.formula: route to exp(-x) when y would be negative

key_findings.cross_entropy_cost_formula: 4N  (N*exp + (N-1)*add_pos + ln + sub; not 6N-1 as previously stated)
key_findings.branch_cut_recommendation: DEML (Option D) for monotonic/exponential targets; complex route (Option C) for oscillatory. Both cost 1n.

superbest_routing_findings.soft_mixture_training: SuperBEST soft mixture (Version B) matches or exceeds pure EML (A) because the operator set spans a richer function class
superbest_routing_findings.cost_penalty_effect: Cost penalty (Version C, λ=0.005) may slightly increase MSE while routing toward cheaper operators
superbest_routing_findings.operator_selection: After training, snap each node to argmax(op_logits) to extract a discrete symbolic tree
superbest_routing_findings.temperature_annealing: Anneal temperature T→0 during training to sharpen operator selection before snapping
